response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

***

She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

***

Reach the goal of supporting local artisans by offering discounts that make their products more accessible. A yard of fabric can be transformed into a beautiful piece of furniture, and with the right tools, even a simple milk jug can be turned into a functional item. The promotional week allows customers to enjoy the benefits of handmade goods while also contributing to the local economy.
